content_markdown:
Container X below has volume $V$ and holds $n$ moles of an ideal gas at kelvin temperature $T$. Container Y has volume $2V$ and holds $3n$ moles of an ideal gas also at kelvin temperature $T$.

The pressure of the gas in X is $P_{\text{X}}$ and in Y is $P_{\text{Y}}$.

The ratio $\frac{P_{\text{X}}}{P_{\text{Y}}}$ is

diagram_metadata.description: Two rectangular containers labeled X and Y. Container X is smaller and contains text: volume V, temperature T, n moles, pressure P_X. Container Y is larger and contains text: volume 2V, temperature T, 3n moles, pressure P_Y.

vibe_explanation: Using the ideal gas law $PV = nRT$, we can express pressure as $P = \frac{nRT}{V}$. For container X, $P_{\text{X}} = \frac{nRT}{V}$. For container Y, $P_{\text{Y}} = \frac{3nRT}{2V}$. Taking the ratio $\frac{P_{\text{X}}}{P_{\text{Y}}} = \frac{nRT/V}{3nRT/2V} = \frac{1}{3/2} = \frac{2}{3}$.
